The transformation described implies that the function \( f(x) \) satisfies \( 2f(x - 2) = f(x) \), which is a functional equation for exponential decay with base \( 2^{1/2} \). Given \( f(0) = 0.1 \), solving the recurrence yields \( f(x) = 0.1 \cdot 2^{-x/2} \). Evaluating at \( x = 10 \) gives \( f(10) = 0.1 \cdot 2^{-5} = 0.1/32 = 0.003125 \), but none of the options match this exactly.

However, reinterpreting the transformation as a **periodic** condition (e.g., \( f(x) = 2f(x-2) \) with \( f(0) = 0.1 \)) leads to \( f(x) = 0
